Description:
CBD DEVELOPMENT SITE

15-17 Liverpool Street presents a rare, inner-city Freehold development opportunity, positioned within the heart of Auckland’s established education precinct.

Comprising 803m² of strategically located land, the site sits within a short walk of both The University of Auckland and Auckland University of Technology, placing it firmly within one of the city’s most consistent demand generators for residential, student accommodation, and mixed-use development.

Located approximately 350–500 metres from Karanga-a-Hape Station (City Rail Link), the property sits within a transport-led regeneration corridor that is reshaping Auckland’s CBD fringe. The convergence of tertiary education demand, major infrastructure investment, and constrained land supply is accelerating intensification throughout the precinct.

Zoned Business - City Centre, with floor area ratio controls removed and a general 50-metre height overlay (subject to planning controls), the site offers exceptional flexibility for high-density redevelopment. Potential schemes include purpose-built student accommodation, build-to-rent, hotel, residential, or commercial development.

Currently generating short-term holding income, the property provides flexibility for intensification in one of Auckland’s most tightly held growth areas.

A genuine blank canvas, underpinned by structural demand drivers and long-term area transformation.

INVESTMENT HIGHLIGHTS:

803m² Freehold landholding
Business - City Centre zoning
General 50 metre height overlay (subject to planning controls)
Located within Auckland’s education precinct
Walking distance to major universities
350–500 metres to Karanga-a-Hape CRL Station
Generating short-term holding income
Multiple high-density redevelopment options

ICONIC. VERSATILE. FUTURE PROOFED.

17 Galatos Street represents a rare, vacant possession, character opportunity for investors or occupiers targeting Auckland's urban intensification. This iconic standalone structure offers a versatile blank canvas for hospitality visionaries, creative converters, or strategic developers.

Positioned in the western CBD just off the historic Karangahape Road ridge, the property is within a short stroll of the nearing completion Karanga-a-Hape Station, which is forecast to handle up to 40,000 daily commuters. This CRL location offers unparalleled accessibility to the education precinct, Ponsonby, and the upper CBD, ensuring the area remains a primary choice for high-density development and commercial activity.

The versatile 578m² building is divided into three spaces across the three levels, each with bathrooms. Features include a stunning 5.9 metre high-stud ground floor with original native timber floors, a character loft space, and a functional basement bar area with separate access. The investment is underpinned by a 350m² Fee Simple landholding. The flexible Business - City Centre zoning ensures significant future-proofing with a development height potential of 31 to 36 metres.

Available with vacant possession (and optional business assets), this high-profile site offers a versatile layout and an atmospheric vibe. It’s a turnkey flagship for owner-occupiers, a prime candidate for repositioning, or a future redevelopment play given the nearing rail network expansion. Positioned in the heart of Auckland’s premier cultural district, it’s ready-made to ride the wave of local growth.

STRATEGIC INVESTMENT - VALUE ADD OR CONVERSION

7 City Road is a 12-level office building occupying a 1,072m² Freehold site within Auckland’s tightly held education precinct.

The property provides approximately 5,186m² of net lettable area and benefits from an exceptional A+ grade seismic rating - a critical differentiator for tenant retention and future-proofed performance. The building generates net passing income of approximately $1,025,483 per annum, presenting clear near-term upside through proactive leasing of existing vacancy.

With an average net rental of approximately $260/m² and a relatively short WALT profile, the asset offers genuine strategic flexibility - enabling investors to capture leasing upside with an estimated fully leased income of circa $1,675,630 per annum, or reposition the building in line with the precinct’s ongoing intensification.

The property is located within close proximity to The University of Auckland and Auckland University of Technology, ensuring sustained occupational demand. Accessibility will be materially enhanced by the completion of Karanga-a-Hape Station (City Rail Link), approximately 350–500 metres away, reinforcing long-term capital growth and redevelopment fundamentals.

Zoned Business - City Centre with a 50-metre height overlay, the site offers scope for vertical expansion or potential conversion to alternative uses, including purpose-built student accommodation, build-to-rent, residential or mixed-use development (subject to approvals).

7 City Road represents a compelling value-add acquisition - combining income today, substantial leasing upside, and multiple future repositioning pathways within one of Auckland’s most resilient growth corridors.

67 Symonds Street represents a compelling combination of secure educational cashflow, scale, car parking, and strategic land value in one of Auckland’s most resilient growth corridors.

Positioned on a prominent north-facing corner with excellent natural light and strong road-front exposure, this modern 217m² showroom presents a rare opportunity in the heart of Rosedale. The elevated site offers an outstanding profile along busy Triton Drive, only 1.5 kilometres from the State Highway 1/State Highway 18 motorway interchange and approximately 15 kilometres to Auckland CBD.

The unit comprises 169m² of ground floor showroom space plus a 48m² mezzanine. It includes polished concrete floors, restrooms on both levels, and a 2.4 metre wide rear roller door for added functionality. Car parking is generous with 43 shared parks plus three exclusive-use spaces.

Located just 200 metres from the new $42 million Rosedale Bus Station, which will connect to the Northern Busway, the property is set to benefit from enhanced accessibility and increased foot traffic.

Built in 2006 by Northbridge Properties and designed by award-winning Herbst Architects, the complex features modern tilt-slab concrete construction. The fully occupied nine-unit development is home to established brands including Icebreaker, Città and The Gateau House.

With flexibility to accommodate showroom, medical, food and beverage, or office uses (subject to council consent), this vacant unit represents a genuine blank-canvas opportunity in a proven commercial location with strong growth fundamentals.

Description:
2.19HA HEAVY INDUSTRIAL – METALLED YARD

Strategically positioned just off Marsden Point Road, this substantial 21,883m² Heavy Industrial site offers a rare opportunity to secure land in one of New Zealand’s fastest-growing port and logistics hubs.

Located minutes from Northport, the property is ideally suited to transport, logistics, storage and yard-based operators. With Northport’s ongoing expansion and the proposed rail connection linking Marsden Point to the national rail network, this precinct is set to become a key upper North Island freight gateway.

The site features approximately 16,000m² – 19,000m² of metalled hardstand, compacted to around 300mm depth and designed for heavy truck and fork hoist movements. The yard has been regularly monitored and maintained, providing confidence for immediate operational use.

With less than approximately 100 hectares of Heavy Industry zoned land in the wider Marsden Point area available for private ownership (from around 420 hectares total), opportunities of this scale are increasingly limited.

PROPERTY HIGHLIGHTS:

21,883m² Heavy Industry zoned land
Approximately 16,000m² – 19,000m² metalled yard
Compacted hardstand for heavy vehicle use
Services to site
Minutes to Northport
Easy access to Ruakaka and State Highway 1
Available immediately

Ideal for owner-occupiers, logistics operators, or investors seeking exposure to the long-term growth of the Marsden Point industrial corridor.
